Data Processing and MNPI Handling Addendum (Japan) between Kisaragi Robotics Co., Ltd. (TSE Prime issuer) and Taikou Advisory KK (委託先), governing APPI “personal data” (including “individual numbers”/My Number) and FIEA material non-public information.
“Personal data” includes employee HR records, board member contact details, and My Number collected for payroll withholding; “individual identification codes” under APPI are treated as sensitive and segregated.
Purpose of processing: employment administration, payroll, equity-based compensation, and acquisition due diligence for a contemplated TOB (公開買付け) of Hoshizora Sensors KK; no secondary use without fresh notice and lawful basis.
Retention: My Number is kept only for the statutory period applicable to tax/withholding documentation and then irreversibly deleted (typically up to 7 years), with secure key wipe and access log review.
DSAR: Data subjects may request disclosure/correction/cessation of use of retained personal data via dsar@kisaragi-robotics.example.jp; we will respond without delay and within two months after verifying identity (government ID check permitted, but do not email copies of My Number).
Cross-border transfers: where support services reside in Singapore, we obtain prior consent or confirm appropriate measures under APPI Art. 28 and provide information on the foreign data protection system; records of third-country transfers are maintained and available on request.
MNPI (未公表の重要事実) under FIEA Arts. 166–167 includes board decisions, TOB/tender-offer facts, mergers, and earnings revisions; such information is confined to a clean team and an insider list (インサイダー名簿) is maintained.
Public-status and timing: until “公表” via TDnet, facts remain non-public; if the board resolves (including a circular resolution) after market close, disclosure will be made on the next business day before the TSE trading session via TDnet in accordance with Timely Disclosure Rules.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; IR or analyst communications may occur only after the relevant matter is posted on TDnet (and, where applicable, EDINET), and meeting decks must be version-locked to the public release.
Blackout windows: directors, officers, employees, and advisors are restricted from trading from quarter-end until earnings are released on TDnet, and during deal-specific blackouts from signing of the NDA until public announcement; extensions may be imposed by the Compliance Office.
Issuer-relative materiality is assessed against likely impact on Kisaragi Robotics’ share price on the TSE Prime Market; unrelated operational updates of third parties (e.g., Sakura Freight KK’s warehouse relocation press release) are not MNPI for Kisaragi.
Public information safe harbor: items already announced on TDnet (e.g., our 2025-02-10 “Notice of Establishment of JV with Tenzan Mobile KK”) may be discussed internally; however, any non-public updates or deviations from those public statements remain MNPI until re-announced.
Access and controls: only Ms. Aoi Ninomiya (General Counsel) and Mr. Daichi Morikawa (Chief Compliance Officer) may authorize secondary use or disclosure; deal files are stored on the restricted “Shirogane” drive with per-user keys and dual-approval export.
Security for My Number: encryption at rest and in transit, strict key management, role-based access, and tamper-evident logs; the processor must notify privacy@kisaragi-robotics.example.jp within 24 hours of any suspected incident.
Audit and training: both parties will retain processing and MNPI access logs for 5 years and complete annual APPI/FIEA training; note that the FY2023 Securities Report filed on EDINET is public and should not be unnecessarily restricted, but draft earnings tables remain non-public until TDnet release.
